description_long: This is a semi-automatic handgun with a metallic silver slide and a dark, textured grip, reminiscent of the classic M1911 design. The frame and slide are constructed from metal, likely steel, with a matte finish, while the grip panels appear to be made of a dark polymer or wood with visible checkering for enhanced hold. The trigger guard is rounded and robust, providing ample space for finger placement, and the trigger itself is skeletonized. The barrel is of standard length, protruding slightly from the slide, and a muzzle flash is visible, indicating the weapon is being fired. The magazine base is flat and flush with the grip, with no visible extensions or finger rests. There are minimal visible markings on the slide, suggesting a clean, utilitarian design without prominent branding or decorative engravings.
description: A metallic semi-automatic handgun with a dark, textured grip and a visible muzzle flash, featuring a clean, utilitarian design and minimal markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the metallic slide, dark grip with checkering, and a prominent muzzle flash at the barrel's end.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the barrel opening, symmetrical slide, and the spread of the muzzle flash.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the slide, grip texture, and the muzzle flash, mirroring the details of the first side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the grip, hammer, and the alignment of the slide and barrel with the muzzle flash extending outward.
attribute_grip_design: The grip features a dark, rectangular panel with pronounced checkering for improved traction and control. It is contoured to fit the hand comfortably, with beveled edges and a slight palm swell. The material appears to be either a high-strength polymer or wood, providing both durability and a classic aesthetic. No visible logos or finger grooves are present, giving the grip a straightforward, utilitarian look. The checkered pattern covers most of the grip surface, enhancing its ergonomic function.
attribute_trigger_guard_style: The trigger guard is rounded and moderately thick, providing ample space for finger placement and gloved use. It has a smooth, untextured finish with no undercuts or squared-off edges, maintaining a traditional profile. The guard is integrated seamlessly into the frame, with no visible reinforcement or accessory rails, emphasizing simplicity and function.
attribute_barrel_type: The barrel is of standard, non-threaded type, extending slightly beyond the slide with a smooth, cylindrical profile. There are no visible ports, compensators, or fluting, and the exterior finish matches the matte metallic look of the slide. The muzzle flash at the tip indicates active firing, but no additional markings or features are visible on the barrel itself.
attribute_slide_markings: The slide appears to be free of prominent markings, engravings, or inscriptions. There are no visible manufacturer logos, model names, serial numbers, or caliber designations on either side of the slide, contributing to a clean and minimalist appearance.
attribute_magazine_base_shape: The magazine base is flat and flush with the bottom of the grip, lacking any extensions, flares, or finger rests. Its design is simple and functional, with no visible logos, texturing, or embellishments, maintaining a streamlined profile with the grip.
